comparison.method: Selected newest article by article date across the BJ category page. Prior article is the immediately preceding June 23, 2026 Trade Journal. June 23 monitoring bucket was HOOD and DUOL; July 6 current monitoring bucket is NTAP only, so NTAP is new_watchlist, there are no continued or rejoined monitoring names, HOOD moved from monitoring to disclosed position, and DUOL was removed from watchlist.

sourceNotes: Logged-in Chrome Profile 2 session was verified on 2026-07-12 HKT; the category page still showed July 6, 2026 as the newest Trade Journal article by article date, ahead of June 23, 2026.

Included tickers only when visible in the July 6 article body text or current-article body images/tables: financial disclosure, holdings table, monitoring table, and body chart images saved under portal/assets/2026-07-06.
Excluded sidebar lesson list, previous/next lesson previews, comments, thumbnails, and pageAssets not tied to the current article body.

Weekly new watchlist/monitoring bucket contains only NTAP. HOOD is not counted as new watchlist because it is now a disclosed position; DUOL is not counted because the article says it is being taken off watchlist.

OHLCV refresh was retried on 2026-07-12 HKT; the latest confirmed Nasdaq regular-session close remains 2026-07-10 for all 11 equities. CPER remains excluded from the normal single-stock screen as a copper index fund.

NTAP closed above the 167.89 flag pivot on July 9 at 171.73 with 5.24M volume, then pulled back to 168.87 on July 10 with 3.30M volume. It remains a research candidate only if the 164-168 breakout shelf holds; renewed strength should show improving volume.
Buy framework applied as Trend -> Base/Pattern -> Pivot -> Volume -> Risk with conservative breakout/pullback entries; Bottom-Up Sell Techniques are included in each sellSystem field.
Educational trade-plan candidates only, not personalized financial advice.

candidates.evidenceOrigin: July 6, 2026 article text, holdings table, and MRVL chart image

candidates.cupHandleRecommendation: 持倉管理 only; no fresh buy until it rebuilds tight support or reclaims range strength.
candidates.trend: Stage 2 but damaged short-term momentum after a sharp range drop
candidates.base: Wide trading range, not a tight VCP
candidates.pivot: No fresh pivot; range support near 245-250 and resistance near 320+ are the visible levels
candidates.trigger: No new entry trigger. Holding trigger is support hold; exit trigger is a close below range support.
candidates.risk: Wide range means fresh stop would be too wide for a clean add.
candidates.stopLoss: For existing holding, reduce/sell on decisive close below the visible support band.
candidates.takeProfit: If it reclaims range highs quickly, scale into strength rather than chase.
candidates.caveat: Author already frames this as support-dependent position management.
candidates.comment: MRVL: position. Financial disclosure lists MRVL long as of July 6; article says MRVL had wild moves, is barely in the trading range, and the author may sell the rest if it breaks/closes below support again. Educational trade-plan candidate only, not personalized financial advice.

sellSystem.initialStop: Existing holding; no new initial stop unless adding. New adds should use a technical stop within roughly 5-10% if possible.
sellSystem.pullbackQuality: Good pullback = tennis-ball action, follow-through, more green candles than red, quick bounce, and holding breakout/support. Bad pullback = drop-like-an-egg action, weak bounce, high-volume selling, or staying below breakout.
sellSystem.earlyExit: Early exit risk rises with 2+ violations: close below 20MA soon after breakout on high volume, three lower lows on above-average volume, weak breakout volume under +50%, immediate negative action, or ER soon without 5-10% cushion.
sellSystem.stopAdjustment: At 2R move stop to breakeven or +2-3%; at 3R continue raising stops under higher lows / 10-20MA.
sellSystem.profitTaking: 持倉管理：用 10/20MA、recent higher low、breakout/support failure 作 sell-into-weakness；若短期急升或接近阻力，分段 sell into strength。
sellSystem.climaxRisk: Watch climax-run risk if price rises >25% in 1-2 weeks, becomes >30% above area of value, goes near vertical, or reverses on exhaustion volume.

stage2Indicators.rs: No independent RS rank fetched; article text + current price action used as qualitative RS evidence only.

candidates.cupHandleRecommendation: July 9 produced a close-confirmed flag breakout with volume; July 10 pulled back near the pivot, so research only if the breakout shelf continues to hold.
candidates.trend: Strong Stage 2: above 50/150/200MA, correct MA order, rising 200MA, near high
candidates.trigger: July 9 closed at 171.73 above the 167.89 pivot on 5.24M volume, about +54.7% versus its then 50-day average. July 10 closed at 168.87 on 3.30M volume; treat this as a breakout retest and require the 164-168 shelf to hold.
candidates.risk: Technical stop is below the July 9 breakout-day low and breakout shelf; exit early if price closes back below the shelf on heavy volume or accumulates 2+ violation signals.
candidates.buyRange: Reference 168.87 close; only on a controlled retest that holds 164-168, or a renewed move above 171.73 with improving volume.
candidates.stopLoss: Below July 9 breakout-day low 164.01 and failed breakout shelf; reference stop 163.50.
candidates.takeProfit: First target near the upper chart objective/resistance around 206; at 2R protect capital and trail if it trends.
candidates.caveat: July 9 breakout volume cleared the +50% threshold, but July 10 pulled back to 168.87 on below-average volume. This is constructive only while 164-168 holds; failure below 163.50 invalidates the setup.
candidates.comment: NTAP: new_watchlist. Article says NTAP has a flag setup and is being watched for a potential breakout; embedded monitoring table lists NTAP as the only monitoring name. Educational trade-plan candidate only, not personalized financial advice.

sellSystem.initialStop: Conditional setup only; initial stop must be below flag/handle low, retest low, breakout support, or key MA invalidation. Ideally keep risk within 5-10%; wider than 10% requires at least 2R.
sellSystem.pullbackQuality: Good pullback = tennis-ball action, follow-through, more green candles than red, quick bounce, and holding breakout/support. Bad pullback = drop-like-an-egg action, weak bounce, high-volume selling, or staying below breakout.
sellSystem.earlyExit: Early exit risk rises with 2+ violations: close below 20MA soon after breakout on high volume, three lower lows on above-average volume, weak breakout volume under +50%, immediate negative action, or ER soon without 5-10% cushion.
sellSystem.stopAdjustment: At 2R move stop to breakeven or +2-3%; at 3R continue raising stops under higher lows / 10-20MA.
sellSystem.profitTaking: Watchlist candidate: at 2R move stop to breakeven or +2-3%; at 3R trail under higher lows/10-20MA. Early Stage 2 names prefer sell into weakness, extended/mature names sell into strength near resistance.
sellSystem.climaxRisk: Watch climax-run risk if price rises >25% in 1-2 weeks, becomes >30% above area of value, goes near vertical, or reverses on exhaustion volume.
